message:
refactor(mol): remove molecule.go (superseded by mol.go)

The simple single-issue instantiation in molecule.go is superseded by
the full DAG-based mol commands. Use 'bd mol bond' for all molecule
instantiation - it handles both single-issue and multi-issue molecules.

Closes: bd-8b0x

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-7yg","title":"Git merge driver uses invalid placeholders (%L, %R instead of %A, %B)","description":"## Problem

The beads git merge driver is configured with invalid Git placeholders:

```
git config merge.beads.driver "bd merge %A %O %L %R"
```

Git doesn't recognize `%L` or `%R` as valid merge driver placeholders. The valid placeholders are:
- `%O` = base (common ancestor)
- `%A` = current version (ours)
- `%B` = other version (theirs)

## Impact

- Affects ALL users when they have `.beads/beads.jsonl` merge conflicts
- Automatic JSONL merge fails with error: "error reading left file: failed to open file: open 7: no such file or directory"
- Users must manually resolve conflicts instead of getting automatic merge

## Root Cause

The `bd init` command (or wherever the merge driver is configured) is using non-standard placeholders. When Git encounters `%L` and `%R`, it either passes them literally or interprets them incorrectly.

## Fix

Update the merge driver configuration to:
```
git config merge.beads.driver "bd merge %A %O %A %B"
```

Where:
- 1st `%A` = output file (current file, will be overwritten)
- `%O` = base (common ancestor)
- 2nd `%A` = left/current version
- `%B` = right/other version

## Action Items

1. Fix `bd init` (or equivalent setup command) to use correct placeholders
2. Add migration/warning for existing users with misconfigured merge driver
3. Update documentation with correct merge driver setup
4. Consider adding validation when `bd init` is run","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-21T19:51:55.747608-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T23:13:40.532368-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T17:24:52.678668-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-89f89fc0","title":"Remove unreachable RPC methods","description":"Several RPC server and client methods are unreachable and should be removed:

Client methods (internal/rpc/client.go):
- `Client.Import` (line 311) - RPC import not used (daemon uses autoimport)

Evidence:
```bash
go run golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode@latest -test ./...
```

Impact: Removes ~80 LOC of unused RPC code","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-28T16:20:02.432202-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T22:58:34.564401-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T22:58:34.564401-08:00","close_reason":"Closed"}

 {"id":"bd-fa2h","title":"🤝 HANDOFF: v0.31.0 released, molecules discussion","description":"Session completed 0.31.0 release and had important molecules discussion.

## Completed
- v0.31.0 released (deferred status, audit trail, directory labels, etc.)
- Fixed lint issues, hook version markers, codesigning
- All CI green, artifacts verified

## Filed Issues
- bd-usro: Rename template instantiate → bd mol bond
- bd-y8bj: Auto-detect identity for bd mail (P1 bug)
- gt-975: Molecule execution support for polecats/crew
- gt-976: Crew lifecycle support in Deacon

## Key Insight
Molecules are the future - TodoWrite is ephemeral, molecules are persistent institutional memory on the world chain. I tried to use TodoWrite for version bump and missed steps (codesigning, MCP verification). Molecules would have caught this.

## Next Steps
- bd mol bond implementation is priority
- Max has gt-976 for crew lifecycle (enables automated refresh mid-molecule)

Check bd ready and gt-975/976 status.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"message","assignee":"beads/crew/dave","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:23:09.889562-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T17:23:09.889562-08:00","sender":"Steve Yegge","ephemeral":true}

 {"id":"bd-fi05","title":"bd sync fails with orphaned issues and duplicate ID conflict","description":"After fixing the deleted_at TEXT column scanning bug (commit 18b1eb2), bd sync still fails with two issues:

1. Orphan Detection Warning: 12 orphaned child issues whose parents no longer exist (bd-cb64c226.* and bd-cbed9619.*)

2. Import Failure: UNIQUE constraint failed for bd-360 - this tombstone exists in both DB and JSONL

Error: "Import failed: error creating depth-0 issues: bulk insert issues: failed to insert issue bd-360: sqlite3: constraint failed: UNIQUE constraint failed: issues.id"

Fix options:
- Delete orphaned child issues with bd delete
- Resolve bd-360 duplicate (in deletions.jsonl vs tombstone in DB)
- Reset sync branch: git branch -f beads-sync main && git push --force-with-lease origin beads-sync","notes":"Fixed tombstone constraint violation bug. When deleting closed issues, the CHECK constraint (status = 'closed') = (closed_at IS NOT NULL) was violated because CreateTombstone didn't clear closed_at. Fix: set closed_at = NULL in tombstone creation SQL.

The sync data corruption (orphaned issues in beads-sync branch) requires manual cleanup: reset sync branch with 'git branch -f beads-sync main && git push --force-with-lease origin beads-sync'","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-13T07:14:33.831346-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T10:50:48.545465-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T07:30:33.843986-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-fom","title":"Remove all deletions.jsonl code except migration","description":"There's deletions manifest code spread across the entire codebase that should have been removed after tombstone migration:

Files with deletions code (non-migration):
- internal/deletions/ - entire package
- cmd/bd/sync.go - 25+ references, auto-compact, sanitize
- cmd/bd/delete.go - dual-writes to deletions.jsonl
- internal/importer/importer.go - checks deletions manifest
- internal/syncbranch/worktree.go - merges deletions.jsonl
- cmd/bd/doctor/fix/sync.go - cleanupDeletionsManifest
- cmd/bd/doctor/fix/deletions.go - HydrateDeletionsManifest
- cmd/bd/integrity.go - checks deletions for data loss
- cmd/bd/deleted.go - entire command
- cmd/bd/compact.go - pruneDeletionsManifest
- cmd/bd/doctor.go - checkDeletionsManifest
- Plus many more

Action: Aggressively remove all non-migration deletions code. Tombstones are the only deletion mechanism now.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T13:29:04.960863-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"task"}

@@ -0,0 +1,40 @@
+# Agent Instructions
+
+This project uses **bd** (beads) for issue tracking. Run `bd onboard` to get started.
+
+## Quick Reference
+
+```bash
+bd ready              # Find available work
+bd show <id>          # View issue details
+bd update <id> --status in_progress  # Claim work
+bd close <id>         # Complete work
+bd sync               # Sync with git
+```
+
+## Landing the Plane (Session Completion)
+
+**When ending a work session**, you MUST complete ALL steps below. Work is NOT complete until `git push` succeeds.
+
+**MANDATORY WORKFLOW:**
+
+1. **File issues for remaining work** - Create issues for anything that needs follow-up
+2. **Run quality gates** (if code changed) - Tests, linters, builds
+3. **Update issue status** - Close finished work, update in-progress items
+4. **PUSH TO REMOTE** - This is MANDATORY:
+   ```bash
+   git pull --rebase
+   bd sync
+   git push
+   git status  # MUST show "up to date with origin"
+   ```
+5. **Clean up** - Clear stashes, prune remote branches
+6. **Verify** - All changes committed AND pushed
+7. **Hand off** - Provide context for next session
+
+**CRITICAL RULES:**
+- Work is NOT complete until `git push` succeeds
+- NEVER stop before pushing - that leaves work stranded locally
+- NEVER say "ready to push when you are" - YOU must push
+- If push fails, resolve and retry until it succeeds
+
